heading: Limitation with respect to acquisition and holding of employer securities and employer real property by certain plans
label: 1107 Limitation with respect to acquisition and holding of employer securities and employer real property by certain plans
notes: References in TextSection 10211(c)(2)(A) of the Revenue Act of 1987 (), referred to in subsec. (d)(5)(C), is set out as a note under , Internal Revenue Code.Public Law 100–203section 7704 of Title 26

children.label: A
children.text: any employer security which is not a qualifying employer security, or

children.label: B
children.text: any employer real property which is not qualifying employer real property.

A plan may not acquire any qualifying employer security or qualifying employer real property, if immediately after such acquisition the aggregate fair market value of employer securities and employer real property held by the plan exceeds 10 percent of the fair market value of the assets of the plan.

children.label: i
children.text: the fair market value of the assets of the plan, determined on , orDecember 31, 1984

children.label: ii
children.text: the fair market value of the assets of the plan determined on .January 1, 1975

children.label: i
children.text: the fair market value of the assets of the plan, determined on such date, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the fair market value of the assets of the plan determined on .January 1, 1975

children.label: A
children.text: After , a plan may not hold any employer securities or employer real property in excess of the amount specified in regulations under subparagraph (B). This subparagraph shall not apply to a plan after the earliest date after , on which it complies with such regulations.December 31, 1979December 31, 1974

children.label: B
children.text: Not later than , the Secretary shall prescribe regulations which shall have the effect of requiring that a plan divest itself of 50 percent of the holdings of employer securities and employer real property which the plan would be required to divest before , under paragraph (2) or subsection (c) (whichever is applicable).December 31, 1976January 1, 1985
Subsection (a) of this section shall not apply to any acquisition or holding of qualifying employer securities or qualifying employer real property by an eligible individual account plan.

children.label: i
children.text: which is not an eligible individual account plan, and

children.label: ii
children.text: to which the requirements of this section apply.

children.label: I
children.text: pursuant to the terms of the plan, or

children.label: II
children.text: at the direction of a person other than the participant on whose behalf such elective deferrals are made to the plan (or a beneficiary).

children.label: ii
children.text: This paragraph shall not apply to an individual account plan for a plan year if, on the last day of the preceding plan year, the fair market value of the assets of all individual account plans maintained by the employer equals not more than 10 percent of the fair market value of the assets of all pension plans (other than multiemployer plans) maintained by the employer.

children.label: iii
children.text: This paragraph shall not apply to an individual account plan that is an employee stock ownership plan as defined in .section 4975(e)(7) of title 26

children.label: iv
children.text: This paragraph shall not apply to an individual account plan if, pursuant to the terms of the plan, the portion of any employee’s applicable elective deferrals which is required to be invested in qualifying employer securities and qualifying employer real property for any year may not exceed 1 percent of the employee’s compensation which is taken into account under the plan in determining the maximum amount of the employee’s applicable elective deferrals for such year.

children.label: C
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “applicable elective deferral” means any elective deferral (as defined in ) which is made pursuant to a qualified cash or deferred arrangement as defined in .section 402(g)(3)(A) of title 26section 401(k) of title 26

children.label: A
children.text: For exemption from diversification requirements for holding of qualifying employer securities and qualifying employer real property by eligible individual account plans, see .section 1104(a)(2) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: For exemption from prohibited transactions for certain acquisitions of qualifying employer securities and qualifying employer real property which are not in violation of 10 percent limitation, see .section 1108(e) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: For transitional rules respecting securities or real property subject to binding contracts in effect on , see .June 30, 1974section 1114(c) of this title

children.label: D
children.text: For diversification requirements for qualifying employer securities held in certain individual account plans, see .section 1054(j) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the fair market value of the assets of the plan determined on such date (disregarding any portion of the fair market value of employer securities which is attributable to appreciation of such securities after ) but not less than the fair market value of plan assets on , orDecember 31, 1974January 1, 1975

children.label: B
children.text: an amount equal to the sum of (i) the total amount of the contributions to the plan received after , and prior to such date, plus (ii) the fair market value of the assets of the plan, determined on .December 31, 1974January 1, 1975

For purposes of this subsection, in the case of an employer security held by a plan after , the ownership of which is derived from ownership of employer securities held by the plan on , or from the exercise of rights derived from such ownership, the value of such security held after , shall be based on the value as of , of the security from which ownership was derived. The Secretary shall prescribe regulations to carry out this paragraph.January 1, 1975January 1, 1975January 1, 1975January 1, 1975

An election under this paragraph may not be made after . Such an election shall be made in accordance with regulations prescribed by the Secretary, and shall be irrevocable. A plan may make an election under this paragraph only if on , the plan holds no employer real property. After such election and before the plan may not acquire any employer real property.December 31, 1975January 1, 1975January 1, 1985

The term “employer security” means a security issued by an employer of employees covered by the plan, or by an affiliate of such employer. A contract to which applies shall not be treated as a security for purposes of this section.section 1108(b)(5) of this title

The term “employer real property” means real property (and related personal property) which is leased to an employer of employees covered by the plan, or to an affiliate of such employer. For purposes of determining the time at which a plan acquires employer real property for purposes of this section, such property shall be deemed to be acquired by the plan on the date on which the plan acquires the property or on the date on which the lease to the employer (or affiliate) is entered into, whichever is later.

children.label: A
children.text: The term “eligible individual account plan” means an individual account plan which is (i) a profit-sharing, stock bonus, thrift, or savings plan; (ii) an employee stock ownership plan; or (iii) a money purchase plan which was in existence on , and which on such date invested primarily in qualifying employer securities. Such term excludes an individual retirement account or annuity described in .September 2, 1974section 408 of title 26

children.label: B
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraph (A), a plan shall be treated as an eligible individual account plan with respect to the acquisition or holding of qualifying employer real property or qualifying employer securities only if such plan explicitly provides for acquisition and holding of qualifying employer securities or qualifying employer real property (as the case may be). In the case of a plan in existence on , this subparagraph shall not take effect until .September 2, 1974January 1, 1976

children.label: C
children.text: The term “eligible individual account plan” does not include any individual account plan the benefits of which are taken into account in determining the benefits payable to a participant under any defined benefit plan.

children.label: A
children.text: if a substantial number of the parcels are dispersed geographically;

children.label: B
children.text: if each parcel of real property and the improvements thereon are suitable (or adaptable without excessive cost) for more than one use;

children.label: C
children.text: even if all of such real property is leased to one lessee (which may be an employer, or an affiliate of an employer); and

children.label: D
children.text: if the acquisition and retention of such property comply with the provisions of this part (other than to the extent it requires diversification, and sections 1104(a)(1)(C), 1106 of this title, and subsection (a) of this section).section 1104(a)(1)(B) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: stock,

children.label: B
children.text: a marketable obligation (as defined in subsection (e)), or

children.label: C
children.text: an interest in a publicly traded partnership (as defined in ), but only if such partnership is an existing partnership as defined in section 10211(c)(2)(A) of the Revenue Act of 1987 ().section 7704(b) of title 26Public Law 100–203

children.label: A
children.text: which is a stock bonus plan which is qualified, or a stock bonus plan and money purchase plan both of which are qualified, under , and which is designed to invest primarily in qualifying employer securities, andsection 401 of title 26

children.label: B
children.text: which meets such other requirements as the Secretary of the Treasury may prescribe by regulation.

A corporation is an affiliate of an employer if it is a member of any controlled group of corporations (as defined in , except that “applicable percentage” shall be substituted for “80 percent” wherever the latter percentage appears in such section) of which the employer who maintains the plan is a member. For purposes of the preceding sentence, the term “applicable percentage” means 50 percent, or such lower percentage as the Secretary may prescribe by regulation. A person other than a corporation shall be treated as an affiliate of an employer to the extent provided in regulations of the Secretary. An employer which is a person other than a corporation shall be treated as affiliated with another person to the extent provided by regulations of the Secretary. Regulations under this paragraph shall be prescribed only after consultation and coordination with the Secretary of the Treasury.section 1563(a) of title 26
The Secretary may prescribe regulations specifying the extent to which conversions, splits, the exercise of rights, and similar transactions are not treated as acquisitions.

For purposes of this section, an arrangement which consists of a defined benefit plan and an individual account plan shall be treated as 1 plan if the benefits of such individual account plan are taken into account in determining the benefits payable under such defined benefit plan.

children.label: A
children.text: on the market, either (i) at the price of the obligation prevailing on a national securities exchange which is registered with the Securities and Exchange Commission, or (ii) if the obligation is not traded on such a national securities exchange, at a price not less favorable to the plan than the offering price for the obligation as established by current bid and asked prices quoted by persons independent of the issuer;

children.label: B
children.text: from an underwriter, at a price (i) not in excess of the public offering price for the obligation as set forth in a prospectus or offering circular filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission, and (ii) at which a substantial portion of the same issue is acquired by persons independent of the issuer; or

children.label: C
children.text: directly from the issuer, at a price not less favorable to the plan than the price paid currently for a substantial portion of the same issue by persons independent of the issuer;

children.label: A
children.text: not more than 25 percent of the aggregate amount of obligations issued in such issue and outstanding at the time of acquisition is held by the plan, and

children.label: B
children.text: at least 50 percent of the aggregate amount referred to in subparagraph (A) is held by persons independent of the issuer; and
immediately following acquisition of the obligation, not more than 25 percent of the assets of the plan is invested in obligations of the employer or an affiliate of the employer.

children.heading: Maximum percentage of stock held by plan; time of holding or acquisition; necessity of legally binding contract
children.label: f Maximum percentage of stock held by plan; time of holding or acquisition; necessity of legally binding contract
A
children.text: no more than 25 percent of the aggregate amount of stock of the same class issued and outstanding at the time of acquisition is held by the plan, and

children.label: B
children.text: at least 50 percent of the aggregate amount referred to in subparagraph (A) is held by persons independent of the issuer.

children.label: A
children.text: has been so held since , orDecember 17, 1987

children.label: B
children.text: was acquired after , pursuant to a legally binding contract in effect on , and has been so held at all times after the acquisition.December 17, 1987December 17, 1987
